user_message: Working on Consolidated Messenger's forecast prep -- need you to draft the follow-up.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sarah Singh <sarah.singh@consolidated.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-02-12 09:07 EST
Subject: URGENT: forecast prep Issue at Consolidated Messenger

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about forecast prep. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Sarah Singh <sarah.singh@consolidated.com>
Date: 2024-01-27 17:38 EST
Subject: Re: forecast prep Discussion - Next Steps

There's been a change in our timeline for forecast prep. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

Meeting: forecast prep Discussion - Consolidated Messenger
Date: 2024-01-09
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on forecast prep from our last session.

[00:08:00] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): On the forecast prep front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

[00:11:00] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:16:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:20:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the forecast prep front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:23:00] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about technical validation. Can we address those?

[00:26:00] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is budget approval.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-K trap (One-page constraint hurts complex meetings); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-K failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-K failure mode (One-page constraint hurts complex meetings)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct forecast prep decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-K -- One-page constraint hurts complex meetings
